disclaimer: Zoning information is provided for preliminary research only. District boundaries, overlays, parcel conditions, interpretations, variances, and recent amendments may affect a property. Confirm all requirements with the controlling jurisdiction before relying on this information.

jurisdiction.source_license: Public — official government data. Verify terms of use with Loudoun County before commercial redistribution.
jurisdiction.ordinance_version: See Municode for current version — a comprehensive zoning ordinance rewrite has been underway
jurisdiction.pilot_notes: Loudoun County is the most important pilot jurisdiction. The Ashburn/Sterling area (Data Center Alley) is the largest data center market in the world by capacity. The county has a defined 'Data Center Facility' use and a Data Center Overlay District (DCOD). Dimensional standards and use classifications in this file are drawn from general public knowledge about Loudoun County zoning and require official verification against the current ordinance.
known_limitations: Zoning polygon geometry is demo only — run data/zoning/scripts/run_zoning_pipeline.py to download real geometry from Loudoun County ArcGIS.
Dimensional standards are approximate and require official verification against the current Loudoun County Zoning Ordinance.
Loudoun County has been revising its Zoning Ordinance — confirm which version is currently effective at https://www.loudoun.gov/zoning before relying on specific section numbers.

PD-IP (Planned Development - Industrial Park) development standards are largely governed by approved development plans and proffered conditions, which vary by parcel. Base ordinance standards are minimums only.
The Data Center Overlay District (DCOD) boundary requires official verification — contact the Loudoun County Department of Planning and Zoning.
Parcel-level data not yet integrated — precise district identification for a specific address requires parcel geometry.
Use table completeness is partial — only the most data-center-relevant districts are included in this pilot.
jurisdiction.notes: Pilot jurisdiction — Phase 1 of zoning intelligence system. Demonstrates complete architecture: jurisdiction config, district records, dimensional standards, use classifications, overlay data, source tracking, and confidence levels.

PD-IP.district_description: Planned development district for high-quality industrial, office, technology, and compatible uses developed under an approved development plan. This is the primary zoning district for data center development in the Ashburn and Sterling portions of Loudoun County. Development standards are governed by both the base ordinance and approved proffer conditions specific to each development plan.
PD-IP.ordinance_chapter: See current Loudoun County Zoning Ordinance
PD-IP.ordinance_section: PD-IP district regulations — verify current section with https://library.municode.com/va/loudoun_county
PD-IP.dc_eligibility_summary: Data Center Facility use authorized in PD-IP with an approved development plan. Most data centers in Loudoun operate in PD-IP under specific proffered conditions. DCOD overlay may apply.
PD-IP.reviewer_notes: Loudoun County's PD-IP zoning is the foundation of Data Center Alley. Every major colocation and hyperscale campus in the Ashburn/Sterling area is built on PD-IP-zoned land. Development plans and proffers are the primary regulatory instruments — they often include site-specific setbacks, noise limits, design standards, and phasing requirements that supersede base ordinance minimums.

minimum_front_setback.original_text: Minimum setback from right-of-way: approximately 40 feet
conditions: From public right-of-way
May be increased by specific development plan
minimum_front_setback.notes: This is an approximate value. Actual PD-IP front setbacks are frequently governed by approved development plans rather than base ordinance.

minimum_side_setback.original_text: Minimum side yard setback: approximately 20 feet; 50 feet when abutting residential

minimum_rear_setback.original_text: Minimum rear yard setback: approximately 20 feet; 50 feet when abutting residential

residential_buffer_setback.original_text: When abutting residential use or zoning: approximately 50-foot buffer
conditions: When abutting residentially zoned property or residential use
residential_buffer_setback.notes: Enhanced buffer requirements may apply in Data Center Overlay District (DCOD) areas.

maximum_building_height.original_text: Maximum building height: approximately 60 feet base; may be modified by approved development plan
conditions: Base ordinance maximum
exceptions: Approved development plan may permit increased height subject to additional review
maximum_building_height.notes: Many data centers in the Ashburn area are 2-4 stories (approximately 30-55 feet). Some have obtained approvals for taller structures.

minimum_lot_area.original_text: No minimum lot area specified in base PD-IP district — governed by development plan
minimum_lot_area.notes: PD districts typically do not have a single minimum lot area — the development plan establishes lot configuration.

conditional_rules.original_text: Side setback increases to approximately 50 feet when the property abuts a residential district or use
Rear setback increases to approximately 50 feet when the property abuts a residential district or use

PD-IP.standards_source_section: PD-IP district regulations — verify current section number

uses.approval_type: Approved development plan with proffered conditions required
conditions: An approved development plan (rezoning with proffers) must be in place for the parcel
Proffered conditions in the development plan may establish site-specific standards including setbacks, noise limits, screening, emergency generator limits, and design requirements
Data Center Overlay District (DCOD) requirements may apply to parcels within the DCOD boundary
uses.supplemental_regulations: Data Center Overlay District (DCOD) requirements apply in designated DCOD areas. See overlays.json for DCOD details.
uses.ordinance_section: Data Center Facility use — verify current section with https://library.municode.com/va/loudoun_county
uses.notes: The PD-IP district with approved data center development plans is the foundation of Loudoun County's data center market. The county's Economic Development office refers to eastern Loudoun as 'Data Center Alley'. Classification as 'moderate' confidence (higher than other standards) because the presence of data centers in PD-IP is a well-documented public fact.

conditions: Per approved development plan
Per approved development plan

uses.notes: Residential uses are generally not permitted in PD-IP, though some proffers have included residential components in mixed-use developments.

uses.approval_type: Special Use Permit or development plan amendment
conditions: May require special use permit depending on the type of facility

dc_analysis.approval_type: Approved development plan with proffered conditions required
conditions: An approved development plan (rezoning with proffers) must be in place for the parcel
Proffered conditions in the development plan may establish site-specific standards including setbacks, noise limits, screening, emergency generator limits, and design requirements
Data Center Overlay District (DCOD) requirements may apply to parcels within the DCOD boundary
dc_analysis.notes: The PD-IP district with approved data center development plans is the foundation of Loudoun County's data center market. The county's Economic Development office refers to eastern Loudoun as 'Data Center Alley'. Classification as 'moderate' confidence (higher than other standards) because the presence of data centers in PD-IP is a well-documented public fact.

I1.district_description: Light industrial district for warehouse, light manufacturing, office, research and development, and compatible uses with minimal external effects. May permit data center use with special approval.
I1.dc_eligibility_summary: Data Center Facility may require a Special Use Permit (SUP) or equivalent approval in I1. Verify with Loudoun County Planning.

minimum_lot_area.original_text: Minimum lot area: approximately 20,000 square feet

minimum_side_setback.original_text: Minimum side setback: approximately 10 feet; 25 feet from residential

conditional_rules.original_text: Side setback increases to approximately 25 feet when abutting a residential district

conditions: Special Use Permit must be approved by the Board of Supervisors
Site-specific conditions will be applied through the SUP process
DCOD requirements may apply

I2.district_description: Heavy industrial district for uses with more significant operational characteristics including noise, traffic, and site coverage. More permissive than I1 for by-right uses.
I2.dc_eligibility_summary: Data Center Facility generally permitted by right in I2. Verify with Loudoun County Planning.

minimum_side_setback.original_text: Minimum side setback: approximately 15 feet; 25 feet from residential

DCOD requirements may apply if within overlay boundary

AR1.district_name: Agricultural Rural — 1 dwelling unit per 30 acres
AR1.district_description: Rural agricultural district covering most of western Loudoun County. Very low density residential use permitted. Designed to protect farmland, rural character, open space, and natural resources. Data center and industrial uses are not permitted uses in this district.
AR1.dc_eligibility_summary: Data Center Facility is not a permitted or similar use in AR1. Rezoning would be required.
AR1.reviewer_notes: AR1 covers the majority of western Loudoun County. The Loudoun County Comprehensive Plan generally directs development to the eastern portion of the county (the Revised Urban Policy Area and the Transition Policy Area), away from AR1-zoned rural areas.

minimum_lot_area_residential.original_text: Minimum lot area for residential use: approximately 3 acres
conditions: For residential use

minimum_lot_area_agricultural.original_text: Agricultural use density: 1 dwelling unit per 30 acres
conditions: For agricultural parcels with residential use
minimum_lot_area_agricultural.notes: The 'density' in AR1 means one dwelling unit may be created per 30 acres. This does not mean a 30-acre parcel is required.

minimum_front_setback.original_text: Minimum front setback: approximately 75 feet from road centerline, or 30 feet from right-of-way, whichever is greater

uses.notes: Data center is not a permitted or similar use in AR1 (Agricultural Rural). Rezoning to an appropriate district would be required. Marked moderate confidence because the agricultural nature of AR1 makes data center exclusion clear.

conditions: At a density of one dwelling unit per 30 acres
SUP required with specific tower and equipment standards

dc_analysis.notes: Data center is not a permitted or similar use in AR1 (Agricultural Rural). Rezoning to an appropriate district would be required. Marked moderate confidence because the agricultural nature of AR1 makes data center exclusion clear.

JLMA-3.district_name: Joint Land Management Area — 3 dwelling units per acre
JLMA-3.district_description: Residential transition zone in the Joint Land Management Area, shared between Loudoun County and adjacent municipalities. Permits residential development at up to 3 dwelling units per acre. Commercial and industrial uses are not permitted.
JLMA-3.dc_eligibility_summary: Data Center Facility is not permitted in JLMA-3. This is a residential district.

maximum_density.original_text: Maximum density: 3 dwelling units per acre

minimum_lot_area.original_text: Minimum lot area: approximately 10,000 square feet

uses.notes: JLMA-3 is a residential district. Data center is not a permitted or conditional use. Rezoning required.

dc_analysis.notes: JLMA-3 is a residential district. Data center is not a permitted or conditional use. Rezoning required.

B2.dc_eligibility_summary: Data Center Facility is not a standard permitted or conditional use in B2. Would require rezoning to an appropriate industrial or planned development district.

PD-OP.dc_eligibility_summary: Data Center Facility eligibility in PD-OP depends on the specific approved development plan and whether it includes data center as a permitted use. Verify with Loudoun County Planning.

DCOD.description: The Data Center Overlay District (DCOD) was adopted by Loudoun County to manage the impacts of data center development in the areas of the county most affected by rapid data center growth, particularly in eastern Loudoun (Ashburn/Sterling area). The DCOD applies additional development standards to Data Center Facilities located within its boundaries, supplementing the base zoning district requirements.
DCOD.geographic_coverage: Portions of eastern Loudoun County, primarily in the Route 28 Technology Corridor and Ashburn area. Exact boundary requires official GIS data from https://www.loudoun.gov/gis
what_it_affects: Sound attenuation standards for mechanical equipment

noise_limit.description: Maximum noise levels at the property boundary (or residential boundary) for mechanical equipment and emergency generators
noise_limit.original_text: Noise standards apply — verify specific decibel limits with the current DCOD ordinance

generator_standards.description: Requirements for emergency generators including fuel storage limits, enclosure, exhaust direction, and testing hour restrictions
generator_standards.original_text: Generator requirements under DCOD — verify with current ordinance

screening_and_design.description: Building and equipment screening requirements to reduce visual impact on surrounding properties
screening_and_design.original_text: Screening and design standards under DCOD — verify with current ordinance

residential_separation.description: Enhanced setbacks or buffers when a data center is located near residential zoning or uses
residential_separation.original_text: Residential separation requirements under DCOD — verify with current ordinance

DCOD.interaction_with_base_zoning: DCOD requirements apply IN ADDITION TO the base district requirements (e.g., PD-IP, I1, I2). When there is a conflict, the more restrictive requirement applies, unless the ordinance specifies otherwise.
DCOD.ordinance_section: Data Center Overlay District regulations — verify current section with https://library.municode.com/va/loudoun_county
DCOD.gis_layer_url: https://www.loudoun.gov/gis — search for DCOD or Data Center Overlay
DCOD.reviewer_notes: The existence of the DCOD in Loudoun County is a well-documented public fact. The county established it in response to significant data center growth, particularly regarding noise and generator impacts on residential neighbors. However, the specific current requirements, exact boundary, and effective ordinance section require official verification.

AIRPORT.description: Loudoun County has airport impact overlay districts related to Dulles International Airport (IAD) and potentially other aviation facilities. These overlays restrict building heights and may affect certain uses in areas within flight paths or noise contours.
AIRPORT.geographic_coverage: Primarily affects eastern Loudoun County near Washington Dulles International Airport. Exact boundaries require official GIS data.
AIRPORT.ordinance_section: Airport overlay regulations — verify with current ordinance
AIRPORT.reviewer_notes: Airport overlays near Dulles may impose height restrictions that affect data center tower heights in some eastern Loudoun locations. Verify for any specific site near the airport.

FLOODPLAIN.description: FEMA-regulated floodplain areas where development is restricted or subject to additional requirements. Data centers typically should not be sited in floodplains due to critical infrastructure concerns.
what_it_affects: Restrictions on below-grade storage of critical equipment
FLOODPLAIN.geographic_coverage: Along stream corridors throughout the county. Exact boundaries from FEMA Flood Insurance Rate Maps (FIRMs).
FLOODPLAIN.ordinance_section: Floodplain overlay regulations — verify with current ordinance
FLOODPLAIN.reviewer_notes: Floodplain constraints are universal across Virginia jurisdictions. Data centers should not be sited in floodplains regardless of zoning. Confidence is moderate because FEMA floodplain regulations are federally mandated.

warnings: Zoning polygon geometry not yet downloaded — zoning map layer will show no district polygons until pipeline runs.
All dimensional standards are approximate — do not cite specific numbers without official ordinance verification.
PD-IP development standards are additionally governed by individual proffer conditions, which are parcel-specific and not captured in this dataset.
Loudoun County has been revising its Zoning Ordinance — the active ordinance version should be confirmed before citing specific section numbers.
DCOD boundary is not yet captured in this dataset — data center eligibility for specific parcels within the DCOD zone may differ from the general district classification.
required_actions: Run data/zoning/scripts/run_zoning_pipeline.py to download real zoning geometry
Verify all dimensional standards against the current Loudoun County Zoning Ordinance at https://library.municode.com/va/loudoun_county
Confirm the DCOD overlay boundary with Loudoun County GIS
Expand district coverage to include remaining Loudoun County zoning districts
Review and update confidence levels after official verification
